response:
Based on your transactions from March to June 2026, you have `8` recurring-style charges totaling **`$675.82` per month** (approximately `$8,110` per year). Since you are self-employed, I've separated these into business tools and personal utilities.

### Business & Professional Software
These charges appear on your **Chase Ink Business Preferred** and represent your core professional stack.

| Merchant | Monthly Amount | Note |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **WeWork Austin** | `$350.00` | Business office membership |
| **Amazon Web Services** | `$82.44` | Infrastructure/hosting |
| **Adobe Creative Cloud** | `$59.99` | Design software |
| **Webflow** | `$39.00` | Website/hosting |
| **Figma Professional** | `$15.00` | Design software |
| **Google Workspace** | `$14.40` | Business email/docs |

### Personal Bills & Utilities
These are paid from your **Capital One 360 Checking** account.

| Merchant | Monthly Amount | Note |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Spectrum Internet** | `$79.99` | Personal internet |
| **Visible Wireless** | `$35.00` | Personal phone |

---

### Review & Optimization Checklist

- [ ] **Check for Annual Billing:** For tools like **Webflow**, **Adobe**, and **Figma**, switching from monthly to annual billing often saves 10–20%.
- [ ] **Audit AWS Usage:** At `$82.44/mo`, it's worth checking if you have any orphaned instances or unattached storage volumes from old client projects that are still accruing costs.
- [ ] **Coworking Flexibility:** Since your income arrives unevenly, check if **WeWork** offers a "Pay As You Go" or lower-tier membership for months when you prefer to work from home.
- [ ] **Self-Employed Health Insurance:** I also observed your **`$415.00`** HealthCare.gov premium. While not a "subscription," ensure you are tracking this for the self-employed health insurance deduction, which can reduce your adjusted gross income (AGI) on your Form 1040.
